title: Research Index
blurb: This directory contains research documents analyzing design decisions, exploring architectural trade-offs, and documenting reasoning for the Swift Institute ecosystem.

documents.topic: Design research for the swift-institute.org "Whitepaper" page: genre taxonomy, load-bearing sections across technical / protocol / foundation sub-types, survey of 12 comparable research-institute methodology pages. Key finding: no surveyed institute publicly names a four-node (research → experiment → code → blog) methodology loop — Anthropic's "Safety Is a Science" three-node loop is the single precedent, on a sub-page, not a dedicated page. Recommendation: Option B — medium essay, 1,500–2,000 words, 5 sections (Thesis / The Loop / Receipts / Why this shape / Relationship to other artifacts), institutional third-person voice. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

Parameter ordering conventions for init and function signatures: closure-last rule, SE-0279 multi-closure lifecycle order, configuration-first-or-last (never middle), struct-over-builder-closure. Four rules promoted to code-surface as [API-IMPL-012] – [API-IMPL-015]. Synthesises Apple API Design Guidelines, SE-0279/SE-0286/SE-0245, TSPL, community writing, stdlib precedents, and zero-violation ecosystem survey. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.
Documentation.docc structure for public alpha launch: principles-first root page, three-layer framing, selective examples over catalogs. Option B (substantial root page) recommended.

DocC renderer + CLI search capabilities on Xcode 26.4.1 toolchain, and Phase 2 merged-site strategy for swift-institute.org. Primary-source verified: sidebar "Filter" + QuickNavigation both title-only (`.exec(t.title)`); `IndexingRecord` full-text shape exists but only emitted by `--emit-digest`, and `docc merge` drops it (MergeAction.swift:52 TODO). Two live tracks: **Option 1** — stay in DocC with Pagefind overlay (1b) for full-text search (2–4h CI wiring); **Option 2** — swift-evolution-style SPA dashboard at /research/ + /experiments/ with MiniSearch/Fuse.js + facet filters (1–2d), DocC retained at /documentation/. Stakeholder feedback ("search is minimum bar, not in DocC") favors Option 2; chrome consistency favors 1b. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide. Cross-refs Experiments/docc-merge-multi-archive-hosting.

Push Event.Source/Driver and Completion resource/Driver/Submission from L3 to L1, leaving only backend factories (+Kqueue/+Epoll/+IOUring/+IOCP) at L3. Forces backend-agnosticism as structural constraint. Full feasibility verified. Tier 2, cross-layer.

C shim placement across five-layer architecture: kernel-primitives shims are redundant (platform packages have identical copies) or misplaced (POSIX shims belong in iso-9945). Recommends zero shims in kernel-primitives, CISO9945Shim in iso-9945, Linux/Darwin Swift code migration to platform packages.

GitHub org migration: 79 repos from swift-standards to 9 body-specific orgs. Local repos already have git history — transfer + remote update + push. 9-phase manifest-driven execution. Converged via Claude–ChatGPT review (4 rounds). Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

TCA26 (ComposableArchitecture 2.0) isolation patterns investigation: 10 patterns analyzed, 5 recommendations for swift-io (non-Sendable closures for confined work, shared Runtime protocol, two-phase drain, explicit nonsending annotations, preserve ~Copyable/~Escapable). Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

TCA26 isolation patterns applied to swift-async-primitives (L1) and swift-async (L3). Per-pattern analysis: F2 (non-Sendable closures) adopted via two-tier architecture; F3 (sync mutation + deferred resumption) adopted and structurally enforced via Decision/Action enums; P4 (nonisolated(nonsending)) fully adopted in primitives, deprecated #isolation pattern in 4 swift-async concrete operators; P7/P9 adopted and exceeded in primitives. 1 actionable recommendation: migrate #isolation to nonisolated(nonsending) in Async.Map/Filter/CompactMap/FlatMap. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

TCA26 isolation patterns applied to swift-kernel, swift-kernel-primitives, and platform packages (Darwin, Linux, POSIX, CPU, System). Per-pattern analysis: F2 (non-Sendable closures) already correctly adopted -- all 8 @Sendable sites are genuinely boundary-crossing (thread spawn, continuation callback), withLock is non-Sendable; F3 (sync mutation + async collection) adopted in Executor runLoop; F1 (isolation unification) not applicable below isolation boundary; P8 (pointer state) adopted more extensively than TCA26 (syscall buffers, ring buffers); P9 (ownership) correctly more advanced (~Copyable Descriptor/Worker, ~Escapable Environment.Entry, consuming join, borrowing syscalls). 6 @unchecked Sendable types all justified. 0 actionable changes -- kernel layer is already clean. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.
Can the Claude Code agent architecture be reimplemented using the Swift Institute ecosystem? Subsystem mapping, gap analysis, new package proposals. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.
Why Task.detached cancellation doesn't propagate through Ownership.Transfer.Cell.take(). Tier 2.
Feasibility of treereduce-swift using tree-sitter-swift grammar for automated test case reduction. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.
Should Paths.Path delegate decomposition to L1 via Span<Char> or raw offset? Tier 2, cross-package.
Could Handle<_Entry> be replaced by Buffer.Arena.Position for Timer.Wheel.ID? Tier 1, cross-package.
Option C from stream-sendable research: ~20 concrete operator types for non-Sendable element flow. Tier 2.
Can Async.Mutex adopt @_rawLayout for inline storage like stdlib Mutex? Tier 2.
Can closure capture consumption be relaxed for non-escaping closures with total consumption guarantee? Would eliminate Slot-per-send overhead. Tier 2, cross-package.

Non-closure peek for ~Copyable elements: `Borrowed<T: ~Copyable>: ~Escapable` wrapper enables `var peek: Borrowed<Element>?` property. Optional<Copyable & ~Escapable> works in Swift 6.3 for computed properties, functions, and coroutines. Key insight: blocker is ~Copyable+Optional (consumption), NOT ~Escapable+Optional (lifetime). 7/7 experiment variants CONFIRMED. SE-0519 `Borrow<T>` converges on same design. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

Canonical ~Copyable ownership transfer: three patterns (always-consume, maybe-consume, borrow-only), layer model (Layer 0/1/2), action enum dispatch, future-proofing table. Codified as [MEM-OWN-010–012] and [IMPL-070]. Audit: 0 violations across Bridge + Channel. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

~Copyable compiler state, transfer patterns, synchronization, bugs, end-state vision. Consolidates noncopyable-ergonomics-compiler-state, noncopyable-ownership-transfer-patterns, noncopyable-closure-capture-relaxation, noncopyable-synchronization-ecosystem-audit, noncopyable-value-generic-deinit-bug. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

~Copyable ergonomics: compiler source investigation of 6 pain points (closure capture, implicit Copyable on extensions, force-unwrap IRGen crash, continuations require Copyable, switch consume, Optional access consuming). 5 of 6 are permanent by design; 1 is a compiler bug. Recommended patterns for Mutex ownership transfer, Optional access, and async handoff. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

Modern concurrency conventions: isolation hierarchy (actors > ~Copyable > sending > Mutex > @unchecked Sendable), Sendable minimization philosophy, swift-io case study (29 @unchecked Sendable audited), 8 conventions, 3-phase migration plan. Synthesizes PF #356/#357/#360, 9 internal research docs, 9 experiments. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

Comprehensive reference for Swift's SE-0458 safety model: `@safe`, `@unsafe`, `unsafe` expressions, 14 unsafe use kinds, expression placement rules, type annotation guidelines, anti-patterns, C interop safety, audit checklist. Derived from compiler source analysis + ecosystem audit experience. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide. Supersedes swift-binary-primitives SE-0458 research.

Swift 6.3 ecosystem migration: 1,390 Package.swift audited. 209 ghost feature flags (`SuppressedAssociatedTypesWithDefaults`), 320 `@inline(__always)` → `@inline(always)`, 36 `_deinitWorkaround` sites pending #86652 verification, zero SE-0502/SE-0505 risk. 3-wave remediation plan. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

Swift 6.3 corpus revalidation status (Tier 2) — [META-006] toolchain revalidation sweep. 290 experiments inventoried. Known fixed in 6.3: #88022, #85743, SE-0452/SE-0446 baselines. Known still broken: #86652 rawlayout deinit, WMO+CopyToBorrow actor enum, try_apply borrow scope. 5 6.4-dev regressions catalogued. Implications for 5 blog pipeline items. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

~Sendable (SE-0518) semantic inventory across swift-kernel-primitives, swift-kernel, swift-io, swift-file-system. 555 types audited. 3 Tier 1 candidates (thread-confined types misrepresented as @unchecked Sendable: IOUring.Ring, IOCP.State, IteratorHandle). 4 Tier 2 debatable. Key finding: ~Copyable already handles most Sendable suppression; ~Sendable gap is thread-confined types. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

Ecosystem-wide audit. **Conversions** (2026-03-24): bare `Cardinal`/`Ordinal` → phantom-tagged, 14 findings, 6-phase plan. **Memory Safety** (2026-03-25): SE-0458 compliance, 27 findings. **Path Type Compliance** (2026-03-31): `Swift.String` as path — 58 findings (10 HIGH). Root cause: decomposition missing at L1. Fix: add to path-primitives, Paths.Path delegates, Kernel.File.Write uses L1 types. 6-phase plan.

Generalized /audit skill design: single `audit.md` per scope, section-per-skill structure, update-in-place, 10 requirement IDs [AUDIT-001–010]. Solves orphan files, version proliferation, naming chaos. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.
Should the "regarding" composition pattern (skill-as-input, e.g. `/audit regarding /implementation`) be generalized to other process skills? Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.
Audit ecosystem for ~Copyable types using actors/atomics/locks for state that ownership already guards. Each is a simplification candidate per [IMPL-063]. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

Complete inventory of data structures across swift-primitives (Memory, Storage, Buffer, Collections) and swift-foundations. Variant system, composition architecture, decision guide. Tier 1, ecosystem-wide.

String/Path type audit across file system stack (301 symbols). Partially implemented: new domain types (Path.Component.Extension, Path.Component.Stem), subtractive String→Path.Component replacement in swift-paths and swift-file-system, callback-based glob in swift-posix. 709 tests pass. Deferred: error wrapping, Kernel.Path in errors (Error requires Copyable). Tier 2, cross-package.

Machine infrastructure extraction (v2.0). Section 1: value-layer (rendering vs machine) — DECISION: status quo, computational models diverge. Section 2: interpreter-layer (parser-machine vs binary-parser run-loop duplication) — RECOMMENDATION: add `Machine.Interpret` module with shared frame/node/failure action enums + `@inlinable` static functions. Full design, consumer shapes, implementation plan. Rendering and serializer NOT recommended for adoption. Deferred naming fixes documented. (Tier 2, cross-package)

Literature review: knowledge encoding as the end-state of software development — 15 intellectual traditions (DDD, formal methods, type theory, ontological engineering, etc.) converging on comprehensive domain encoding vs. isolated problem-solving. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.
Tiered research methodology framework (RES-020-026)

Inventory of `actor` declarations across swift-primitives, swift-standards, and swift-foundations (2026-04-13). Distinguishes test actors (boundary verification) from production actors (state ownership). No `distributed actor` declarations.

Review of implementation work for the dependencies, witnesses, and parsers ecosystem adoption audits — compares research recommendations against actual commits across swift-primitives, swift-standards, and swift-foundations
Should remaining Tagged+Affine operators be unified via Domain? (Tier 1) — completeness check after Phase 2

Agent-to-agent handoff in Claude Code: failure mode analysis (handoff paradox — degraded agent writes the handoff), SBAR-derived template, progressive capture, file-based handoff at `HANDOFF.md`, token budget (500–1500), timing triggers, acceptance protocol. Prior art: medical SBAR, ATC handover, NASA MER, industrial shift ops, OpenAI Swarm, Continuous Claude v3, "lost in the middle" research. Recommends `/handoff` skill design. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

Agent-to-agent supervision in Claude Code: principal-supervises-subordinate posture distinct from `/handoff`. Prior art: Anthropic orchestrator-worker + multi-agent research, LangGraph supervisor, CrewAI hierarchical, AutoGen GroupChatManager, MetaGPT SOP, Semantic Kernel group chat, Magentic-One Task/Progress ledgers, OpenAI Swarm. Distills 7 axes (scope, strictness, Q/A, drift, intervention, termination, escalation), reverse-engineers swift-io HANDOFF.md "Supervisor constraints #1–#4" as lived example, recommends 4–6 entry typed ground-rules block. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide. Recommends `/supervise` skill design with [SUPER-*] requirement IDs.

AI context reduction via type system tooling (Tier 2) — 4 options (root Package.swift for CCLSP workspace indexing, symbol graph pipeline for batch API inventory, hybrid CCLSP for interactive composition, signature shape meta-analysis). Recommends phased: symbol graph pipeline first (proven, high-value), then scoped workspace Package.swift, then meta-analysis scripts, then hybrid workflow. Empirical validation: symbol graph extraction works, CCLSP workspace search blocked by missing root manifest.

Apple swift-http-api-proposal: ownership and concurrency patterns (Tier 3). Five patterns catalogued with source excerpts: `consuming sending` on closure parameters, `~Copyable & ~Escapable` middleware, AsyncWriter providing OutputSpan, `withClient` scoped resource, SendableMetatype usage.

Apple HTTP middleware chain isolation (Tier 3) — ownership transfer (`consuming Input`) replaces shared mutable context as the synchronization mechanism for `~Copyable & ~Escapable` middleware values.
Apple HTTP AsyncWriter OutputSpan pattern (Tier 3) — writer hands out `inout OutputSpan<WriteElement>` for zero-copy append, replacing caller-allocates-then-writer-copies.
Apple HTTP `DefaultHTTPClient.withClient` scoped pattern (Tier 3) — combines typed throws, `~Copyable` return, and `borrowing` access for resources requiring cleanup.

Comprehensive audit of ASCII domain ownership refactoring (Steps 0-5) and alignment with transformation domain architecture (Tier 2) — implementation correctness, research document accuracy, ecosystem consistency. Overall verdict: CLEAN. 10 stale documentation inaccuracies, 3 forward-looking design questions.

Adversarial review of ascii-parsing-domain-ownership v4.0→v4.1 (Tier 2, v2.0) — two review rounds. v1.0: 8 claims, 4 revisions recommended. v2.0: 6 verdicts upgraded QUALIFIED→CONFIRMED after v4.1 revisions. Two remaining factual corrections: (1) LEB128 serialization EXISTS (`[UInt8](leb128:)` in binary-primitives) — "one-directional" characterization is wrong; (2) standards `.Parse`/`.Parser` convention split 22/25 — no dominant convention. New "domains as namespaces" principle QUALIFIED (sound but degenerate-case criterion needs refinement). Four-protocol design (Protocol, Serializer, Printer, ParserPrinter) confirmed. Overall: v4.1 holds with corrections.

ASCII parsing domain ownership (Tier 2, v4.2) — design principle: domains as namespaces, capabilities as nested types. `ASCII.Decimal.Parser<Input, T>` (LEB128 degenerate: simple serialization doesn't need namespace siblings). Four capability protocols (Protocol, Serializer, Printer, ParserPrinter). Standards convention split 22/25 Parse/Parser — `.Parser` adopted as design choice. New package: `swift-ascii-parser-primitives` (L1, Tier 18). L3 deferred. Machine IR in swift-ascii (namespace-corrected). `Binary.ASCII.Serializable` scoped out. 6-phase migration. Post-adversarial review v2.0.

ASCII serialization migration (Tier 2) — full migration plan for ~83 `Binary.ASCII.Serializable` conformers across 16+ standards packages. No replacement domain protocol. Separate Parser + Serializer per type. Constrained convenience extensions on Parseable/Serializable. Per-type CustomStringConvertible opt-in. 8-phase rollout aligned with parser adoption. Zero external generic constraints — clean migration. Full cleanup: delete protocol, Wrapper, RawRepresentable.

Should Async.Stream require Element: Sendable? Tier 2 investigation. 4 options: status quo, non-Sendable Stream, two-tier concrete+erased, dual types. Preliminary: Option C (two-tier) strongest — concrete operators for isolation-preserving composition, Stream as explicit erasure/concurrency boundary. Synthesizes stream-isolation-preservation experiment, 3 prior research docs.

Benchmark implementation conventions (Tier 2) — 7 patterns extracted from swift-io case study: suite hierarchy, tiered .timed() config, backtick naming with workload params, clean namespace enums, Test Support fixtures, byte-identical WorkSimulator for comparison, Benchmarks/ sub-package structure. Prepares ground for benchmark-implementation skill.

Benchmark inline strategy (Tier 2) — parameter ownership between `.timed()` and `#benchmark`/`measure {}`, and whether `#benchmark` should parallel `#snapshot`'s inline source rewriting. Recommends Option E: unified `#benchmark` macro with inline documentary results (not assertion). Key insight: benchmarks are non-deterministic, so inline values are documentation, not baselines.

Guaranteeing serial, deterministic benchmark execution across the ecosystem (Tier 2) — root cause: structural nil nodes in test tree dispatch all suites with `.automatic`. Two-track recommendation: (1) single `@Suite(.serialized)` root convention (already proven in 8 rendering targets), (2) deferred `Testing.main(concurrency:)` API. Affects 10+ performance test targets. Moved from swift-tests per [META-017].
Where measurement/metric types belong in the ecosystem (bottom-up). Scope: ecosystem-wide.
Should benchmark result data (`.benchmarks/` JSONL files) be committed to git? Tier 2. Prior art: 5 storage strategies surveyed (nothing, thresholds, curated, gh-pages, external service).

Benchmarking strategy (Tier 2) — bridge `.timed()` with `Test.Benchmark.measure {}` via `Dependency.Scope` for setup isolation + full diagnostics. Single trait, progressive disclosure. Run history (JSONL) and AI export (run-level summary JSON) as phases 2–3.
Zero-bit scanning infrastructure for Bit.Vector / Bit.Vector.Static (Tier 2) — mirrors .ones with .zeros, enables Storage.Pool.Inline.allocate() to replace raw loop
Blog pipeline stall check — 32 items checked, 18 stalled >30 days. [META-019] Phase 9.

`Async.Callback<Value>` nonsending replacement design (Tier 2, v3.1) — `await callback()` via `callAsFunction(isolation:)`, no `Sendable` constraint on Value, plain sync transforms. Issue #83812 workaround: `await self()` in map/flatMap. Replaces existing CPS Callback (Option E). **v3.1**: stdlib deprecated `isolation:` parameter pattern — future migration to `nonisolated(nonsending)` on method recommended.

Canonical and witness capability attachment (Tier 2) — protocol for single canonical (`Parseable`, `Serializable`, `Codable`) + witness properties for alternatives. 4 options evaluated. Option C decided (canonical protocol + witness alternatives). Empirically validated (10/10 CONFIRMED). Three alternative patterns: parameterized factory, domain-owned types, descriptive properties. All questions resolved.

Architecture analysis of the Kuberwastaken/claurst specification documents (Tier 3) — eight specification documents reverse-engineered from the Claude Code TypeScript source (~1,902 files, ~800K LOC) into a Rust port. Catalogues query loop, tools system, agents, services, hooks, and special subsystems.
Implementation pattern analysis of Claurst, a Rust port of Claude Code (Tier 3, source as of 2026-04-01) — 11-crate Cargo workspace, decomposition patterns, dependency graph rooted at `cc-core`.

Clock Dependency.Key placement (Tier 2) — where to place `@Dependency(\.clock)` integration providing type-erased clock with mode-aware defaults (ContinuousClock production, Clock.Immediate tests). Requires access to both swift-dependencies (L3) and swift-clock-primitives (L1).

Clock.Continuous static now convenience and accuracy audit (Tier 2, v2.0) — 16 files using stdlib ContinuousClock.now. Three findings: (1) CRITICAL: Darwin uses `CLOCK_MONOTONIC` (buggy, can jump backwards) instead of stdlib's `CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW`; (2) add `static var now: Instant` in platform packages; (3) optional `clock_gettime_nsec_np` optimization. Performance analysis confirms `Self().now` matches stdlib.
Claude-ChatGPT collaborative discussion workflow

Comparative analysis: apple/swift-system-metrics vs Swift Institute ecosystem (Tier 1) — gap analysis, design pattern comparison, 4 priority recommendations: POSIX rusage/rlimit wrappers, Darwin proc_pidinfo, Linux procfs utilities, Swift 6 feature flag audit
Comparative analysis: swift-array-primitives vs swift-io Array usage — L1 Primitives × L3 Foundations scope, six modules re-exported through Array_Primitives
Comparative analysis: Bitset/Bit-Vector primitives vs swift-io bit-level patterns — swift-bitset-primitives and swift-bit-vector-primitives inventory and cross-package usage
Comparative analysis: swift-buffer-primitives vs swift-io buffer/pool patterns — L1 buffer type catalog and L3 consumption mapping
Comparative analysis: swift-dictionary-primitives vs swift-io Dictionary usage — replacement-opportunity analysis for slab-backed Dictionary and Dictionary.Ordered
Comparative analysis: Hash-Table-Primitives vs swift-io hash-based structures — hash-table-primitives catalog and cross-package utilization
Comparative analysis: swift-heap-primitives vs swift-io heap usage (Tier 2) — heap-primitives API surface mapped onto two deadline-scheduling subsystems in swift-io, friction point evaluation
Comparative analysis: List/Stack primitives vs swift-io patterns — dependency-utilization-audit of swift-list-primitives and swift-stack-primitives
Comparative analysis: swift-queue-primitives vs swift-io Queue usage — replacement-opportunity analysis for seven queue modules
Comparative analysis: swift-set-primitives vs swift-io Set usage — L3 consumption of L1 set primitives (Set.Ordered, Set.Ordered.Static, Set.Ordered.Bounded)
Comparative analysis: swift-slab-primitives vs swift-io slab/pool usage — four-module slab primitives inventory and cross-package utilization

Comparative analysis: Apple swift-testing vs Swift Institute swift-testing (Tier 3) — module architecture, macro system, type safety, trait system, runner design, capability gaps. 8-dimension comparison with formal semantics, Cognitive Dimensions validation. Novel contributions: layered decomposition, typed throws, ~Copyable registry. Key gaps: expression decomposition, parameterized tests.

Keyed tree primitive design (Tier 3, v3.0) — SLR (34 sources: Fredkin, Hinze, Okasaki, Connelly-Morris, Bagwell, Prokopec), formal semantics, 5-option analysis. Existing tree types cannot be upgraded (child addressing baked into node layout). Literature: tries are maps, but swift-testing's Graph is a keyed tree (structural ops: subgraph, zip, recursivelyApply). `KeyedTree K V` subsumes `Trie K U` when `V = U?`. Recommends `Tree.Keyed<Key, Value>` in swift-tree-primitives.

Comparative analysis: Tree/Graph primitives vs swift-io patterns — minimal overlap, no replacement opportunities. Deadline scheduling and sharded registries already well-served by Heap and Dictionary.

Compiler PR handoff: fix mark_dependence simplification for ~Escapable coroutine yields (swiftlang/swift#88022). Root cause traced via SIL dumps: PredictableDeadAllocationElimination + SILCombine multi-pass interaction. Tier 2.

@concurrent expansion audit — with NonisolatedNonsendingByDefault enabled, audit of which nonisolated async functions genuinely execute on a different executor and need `@concurrent` across swift-io, swift-kernel, swift-async-primitives, swift-async

Cross-package integration strategies (Tier 3, v2.0) — taxonomizes intra-package isolation (Problem 1, Core extraction) vs cross-package optional integration (Problem 2). 5 strategies evaluated. **Decision: Strategy D (SE-0450 trait-gated targets)**. Validated with swift-dependencies clock integration. Trait gates dependency resolution; zero repo proliferation; explicit consumer opt-in. Rust cargo features model.

Can W3C CSS Scale be expressed via Dimension Primitives Scale? (Tier 1) — domain mismatch analysis: CSS property value (serialization) vs mathematical transformation (computation). Variable arity, dependency cost, layer architecture all favor independence with optional conversion.

Dependencies ecosystem adoption audit (Tier 1) — identifies where across the ecosystem ad-hoc patterns can be replaced by swift-dependency-primitives (L1) and swift-dependencies (L3) formal dependency injection

Dependency/witness store coherence (Tier 2, v2.1) — L1 `Dependency.Scope` and L3 `Witness.Context` maintain separate @TaskLocal stores; values invisible across boundary. Full investigation: dependency chain analysis, conformance census (18 L1 keys, 8+ L3 keys), 5 options evaluated. `SuppressedAssociatedTypes` unblocks Option D (protocol refinement). Recommends 3-phase: Phase 1 mode propagation (eliminates dual scope push, 2 files); Phase 2 protocol refinement (`Witness.Key: Dependency.Key`, resolves name shadowing, 3 files); Phase 3 L1 key bridge (full cross-store visibility, 4 files).

Developer tool package architecture (Tier 3) — where tools sit in five-layer architecture, manifest parsing strategy (dump-package JSON + Codable), formal dependency graph semantics, per-target classification algorithm (UNUSED/REDUNDANT/NEEDED), type architecture. Case study: redundant dependency detection. Prior art: 6 ecosystems surveyed (Rust, Node, Go, Swift, JVM, Python). REDUNDANT detection is novel — no existing tool in any ecosystem performs it.
Cross-domain scaling factors and type-safe conversion

Documentation-Research-Experiments integration (Tier 2) — connecting three currently disconnected skills (/documentation, /research-process, /experiment-process) and evaluating DocC-navigable research/experiment artifacts
Documentation skill design — inline DocC + .docc catalogue conventions for the /documentation skill. Separate from /readme skill.

Prior art survey supporting `domain-first-repository-organization.md` (Tier 3) — multi-language project repository conventions (gRPC, Kubernetes, Rust, etc.), package registry interactions with org identity, academic taxonomies for computational domains.

Domain-first vs language-first repository organization (Tier 3) — 9 multi-language projects surveyed, 5 organizational models evaluated, formal analysis, `reality-` prefix, converged via Claude–ChatGPT collaborative review. Recommends Model D (Hybrid).

Converged plan: structural type primitives decomposition (Tier 2) — three independent macros (@Dual categorical duality, @Defunctionalize Reynolds 1972 call algebra, @Witness DI/test/observation) with shared enum infrastructure in a neutral support package. Contracts, invariants, and generated type names defined.
Collaborative discussion transcript: structural type primitives decomposition — Claude + ChatGPT. Option A (separate macros) converged. Duality vs defunctionalization preserve different invariants.

Dual-mode Package.swift for development + publication (Tier 2) — cross-ecosystem survey (Rust/pnpm/Go/Haskell), 4 SPM options evaluated. Recommends env-var conditional (`getenv`, Foundation-free, Apple-pattern `SWIFTCI_USE_LOCAL_DEPS`) + layer-level lockstep versioning.

Effects ecosystem adoption audit (Tier 1) — identifies adoption opportunities for swift-effect-primitives (L1) and swift-effects (L3) algebraic effects across the ecosystem. Several packages already use Effect.Protocol; systematic audit for broader adoption.

Case study: swift-emailaddress-standard (Tier 1, v2.0) — traces Domain→EmailAddress→Email chain. Shape B (standard-first, multi-spec): substantive convergence (11 files), 9+ direct consumers, strong encapsulation. Keep in swift-standards/. Canonical example of domain-concept sub-layer.

Enum infrastructure primitives (Tier 2) — shared enum codegen (prisms, discriminants, extraction) duplicated across @Dual, @Defunctionalize, @Witness macro implementation targets. Recommends extraction into a neutral support package.

Test expectation failure reporting bridge (Tier 2) — two problems: (1) `record(failing:...)` silently drops failures under Apple's Swift Testing runner (`Collector.current` is nil), (2) inline snapshot rewriter never fires (depends on `Test.Runner.postRunActions`). 4 failure-reporting options + 3 rewriter-lifecycle options evaluated. Recommends Option A (`#if canImport(Testing)` fallback with nil-collector guard) + Option R1 (`atexit` handler, lazily registered on first `State.register()`, safely composable with `postRunActions` via destructive `drain()`). Full recording-mode lifecycle verified.
Experiment revalidation inventory — BUG REPRODUCED/REFUTED experiments for retesting on toolchain upgrade. [META-019] Phase 7.

Expression decomposition for `#expect`/`#require` macros (Tier 2) — 4 options (full Apple mirror, operator overloads, minimal macro decomposition, hybrid phased). Recommends Option D (hybrid): Phase 1 binary operator decomposition using existing `Test.Expression.Value`/`Test.Expectation.Failure` types (zero primitives changes, ~8 files); Phase 2 extends to function calls, property access, casts. Typed throws [API-ERR-001], no Mirror. Closes #1 usability gap from comparative analysis.
~~Feature flags: AddressableParameters, AddressableTypes, BorrowInout~~

Consolidated feature flags assessment — 9 experimental Swift features (ownership, accessors, compile-time values). Compiler maturity + ecosystem impact. All WAIT except Reparenting (enable when stable) and UnderscoreOwned/BorrowInout (SKIP).
~~Feature flags compiler source analysis~~
~~Feature flags: CompileTimeValues, StructLetDestructuring, Reparenting~~
~~Feature flags: CoroutineAccessors, BorrowAndMutateAccessors, UnderscoreOwned~~

File/Directory/Path consumer call-site inventory across all repos importing File_System, File_System_Primitives, Paths, or Path_Primitives. Construction patterns, API surface usage, type flow analysis. Tier 2, cross-package.
File/Path type unification audit for swift-paths and swift-file-system — type surface inventory, ExpressibleByStringLiteral tension with throwing init. Tier 2.

Foundation-free time and locale in swift-translating (Tier 2) — eliminating Foundation from swift-translating's core modules using swift-primitives Foundation-free alternatives for Date, DateComponents, DateFormatter, Locale, and String.folding

Dependency utilization audit of swift-io and swift-kernel (Tier 1) — cross-referenced against [IMPL-*] and [INFRA-*] skills. Kernel: clean. IO: 2 actionable improvements (Tagged Flag forwarding, reserveCapacity overload).

Git subtree (Apollo pattern) publication evaluation (Tier 2, v2.0) — Apollo iOS production analysis, splitsh-lite. **Not applicable**: repos are already separate (git submodules, not monorepo). Actual problem: path→URL manifest transformation + version coordination across 348 repos. CI manifest rewriting or release branch recommended.
Gitignore sync strategy (Tier 2) — canonical/local-override .gitignore convention, drift remediation across ~370 repositories, sync reference pattern

Handler-witness conceptual identity (Tier 1) — are effect handlers and protocol witnesses the same concept? 3 options (identity, separation, unification). Decision: Option C — a witness is a degenerate handler (resumes exactly once, synchronously). Shared DNA: dictionary-passing, proof objects, `Dependency.Key` substrate. Operational difference (continuation) justifies separate types. No code changes.
Handoff prompt for ASCII domain ownership and transformation architecture audit (Tier 2) — session handoff documenting implementation steps and providing context for audit continuation
Agent handoff prior art notes — actionable patterns from medical SBAR, ATC handover-takeover, and other domain handoff protocols. Supporting material for agent-handoff-patterns.md.
New implementation skill: expression patterns, typed arithmetic, boundary overloads (Tier 2) — supersedes anti-patterns
Intent over mechanism as foundational axiom; expression-first code style (Tier 3)

IO prior art survey + swift-io design audit (Tier 2, Discovery) — 15 IO systems surveyed (Rust, Go, Java, .NET, Zig, OCaml, Haskell, SwiftNIO, Swift System, epoll/kqueue/IOCP/io_uring, libuv), 4-tier concept necessity spectrum, then 72 swift-io concepts mapped against it. 78% Tier 1-2, 14% Tier 3, 7% Tier 4, 1 novel (IO.Executor.Slot). Verdict: correct and necessary, zero gratuitous abstractions.

Entry point API for isolation-preserving AsyncSequence operators (Tier 2, v2.0) — sync-closure overloads `(Element) -> Output` win overload resolution over stdlib's `@Sendable (Element) async -> Output` without any wrapper or accessor. Transform enum (`.sync`/`.async` cases) + direct `next(isolation:)` defeats conformance trap. All 31 tests pass.

Issue investigation best practices: literature study and comparative analysis (Tier 2) — universal pipeline (minimize, bisect, dump IR, fix, regression test) across Rust, GCC, LLVM, Swift ecosystems. Evidence-based practices for the issue-investigation skill.

Localization architecture analysis (Tier 1, v2.0) — unified Foundation-free localization across the five-layer stack. Single `@Dependency(\.locale)`, L2 CLDR-derived locale data, formatters with DI-reading inits, L2 Language wrapping ISO 639. Collaborative discussion converged.
Collaborative discussion transcript: localization architecture for Swift Institute — Claude + ChatGPT. Single dependency, L2 locale data, formatter DI design.

Layout.Stack adaptation in pdf-rendering (Tier 2) — VStack/HStack typealiases to same type cause footgun. First-principles analysis: axis is data, diagonals correctly unrepresentable, spatial names belong in layout-primitives. Decision: `PDF.Stack(.horizontal) { }` with `.horizontal`/`.vertical` on `Axis<2>` in layout-primitives, default `.vertical`, remove VStack/HStack typealiases.

Line box geometry unification (Tier 2) — duplicated half-leading computation in `CSS.LineBox` (swift-pdf-html-rendering) and `Style.Resolved.Line` (swift-pdf-rendering). 4 options: layout-primitives, text-primitives, new typography-primitives, status quo. Recommends `Layout.Line.Box` in layout-primitives — zero dependency cost, clear namespace, raw-height init (no font-metric coupling). Half-leading is CSS-specific but the geometric operation is general. `belowBaseline` stored, `aboveBaseline` replaces ambiguous `baselineOffset`. Cap-height centering identified as secondary unification candidate.

Macro composition architecture (Tier 2) — definitive architectural relationship between @Dual, @Defunctionalize, and @Witness. Five packages (swift-dual, swift-defunctionalize, swift-witnesses, swift-dependencies, swift-dependency-primitives), PointFree equivalents mapped, boundaries and composition rules established.

Swift bug report: `mark_dependence` CopyPropagation interaction (Tier 2) — `~Escapable` + `@_lifetime(borrow)` generates `mark_dependence` classified as `PointerEscape`, causing partial canonicalization bailout and double `end_lifetime` on `~Copyable ~Escapable` values across control flow joins. Standalone reproducer from copypropagation-nonescapable-mark-dependence experiment.
Ideal measurement type system from first principles (top-down). Scope: ecosystem-wide.

Memory domain cross-package inventory (Tier 2) — exhaustive audit of Memory and Kernel.Memory across swift-memory-primitives, swift-kernel-primitives, swift-iso-9945, swift-windows-primitives, swift-kernel, and swift-memory. Finding: no re-implementation detected. Kernel.Memory properly wraps Memory.Address and Memory.Alignment via phantom-tagged typealiases; all OS-specific types (Map, Lock, Shared, Page) are genuinely new. Dependency direction correct throughout.
Minimal type body with extensions pattern
Modularization audit ecosystem-wide summary (Tier 2) — 199 packages (132 primitives + 67 foundations), 117 FAIL, 34 REVIEW, 49 COMPLIANT. Top 10 findings.
Modularization audit swift-primitives delta report (Tier 2) — zero Package.swift changes since 2026-03-14 audit. Top 5 findings verified still open.
Cross-repo Mutex inventory across swift-primitives and swift-foundations — Synchronization.Mutex, Async.Mutex, Kernel.Thread.Mutex usage in Sources/ directories.
Nesting protocols in generic types (Tier 3) — compiler source analysis, SE-0404 gap, no feature flag exists; `Buffer.Arena.Protocol` blocked by `isGenericContext()`
Cross-language literature study (Tier 3) — 52 sources across Scala/Rust/OCaml/Haskell/C++/type theory; validates "without capture" as ideal design, not just minimum; three-tier recommendation
Nested testing package flattening (Tier 1) — reducing deeply nested test paths from `Tests/Testing/Tests/` stutter to flatter structure

Nested package structure for performance + snapshot testing with swift-testing (Tier 1) — 4 options evaluated. Decision: Option A, single nested package at `Tests/Testing/` with multiple test targets. `#Tests` macro recommended default. Skill renamed `testing-performance` → `testing-institute` [INST-TEST-*].
Next steps: dependencies ecosystem adoption — status after first implementation pass. 12 done, 1 reverted then re-applied, 0 MEDIUM and 9 LOW remaining.

Next steps: parsers ecosystem adoption — status after first implementation pass. Parser.ASCII.Integer infrastructure added, 18 packages with new parsers (PARTIAL), 1 fully done (RFC 6750), 57+ items not started.

Next steps: witnesses ecosystem adoption — all phases COMPLETE. 5 macro improvements, task-local unification, Witness.Key refined from Dependency.Key, all conformances and registrations verified in source.

Non-Sendable strategy with isolation-based thread safety (Tier 2) — successor to sendable-in-rendering-and-snapshot-infrastructure.md. Strategy no longer constrains Value: Sendable; now designing isolation-based thread safety for Strategy itself.

~Copyable value-generic deinit bug (Tier 2) — Swift compiler (#86652 variant) fails to synthesize member destruction for ~Copyable structs with cross-package, value-generic, @_rawLayout stored properties. Two sub-bugs: (A) deinit body skipped without reference-typed property, (B) automatic member destruction not synthesized. Workaround: `AnyObject?` + manual `remove.all()` via mutable pointer. Applied to 21 types across 9 packages. Simplified reproduction does not trigger (requires @_rawLayout).

~Escapable readiness, storage patterns, borrowed access, lifetime annotations, gaps. Consolidates nonescapable-readiness-assessment, noncopyable-peek-escapable, nonescapable-storage-mechanisms, lifetime-annotation-escapable-swift-6.3 (from swift-primitives). Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

~Escapable readiness assessment for async ecosystem (Tier 2, v2.3.0) — 8 candidates, 3 HIGH. Resumption ~Escapable attempted + **REVERTED** (downstream collections require Escapable). Entry/Queue blocked by UnsafeMutablePointer. Gap A/B closure blockers persist. **No async type currently ~Escapable.** Heap-backed container blocker cascades to all collection usage.

~Escapable storage mechanisms (Tier 2, v1.2.0) — exhaustive investigation: 17 experiment variants (9 PASS, 11 BLOCKED). Enum-based variable-occupancy storage works (2-8 elements). @_rawLayout layout-vs-access gap. Pointer Escapable blocker confirmed (SE-0465 deferred). Resumption deployment **REVERTED** — demonstrates cascade consequences of blocker.

Nonsending adoption audit of async ecosystem (Tier 2, v1.2.0) — 52 `@Sendable` sites inventoried (14 primitives, 38 stream). Stream operators NOT viable (0 sites). `Async.Callback.Isolated` identified as primary opportunity (6 sites). 4 language blockers resolved by experiment.

Nonsending compiler patterns (Tier 2, v1.0.0) — Discovery: how the Swift compiler/stdlib uses nonisolated(nonsending). Key finding: stdlib deprecated `isolation:` parameter in favor of `nonisolated(nonsending)` on functions. Our `Async.Callback.callAsFunction(isolation:)` uses the deprecated pattern. Also: `sending` return types, no `@concurrent` in stdlib, implicit actor parameter at SIL level, conformance trap evidence.

`nonisolated(nonsending)` `callAsFunction` inference quirk (Tier 1) — sugar syntax `await callback()` infers result as `() -> Value` (method reference) instead of `Value`. Explicit `.callAsFunction()` or type annotation resolves it. Runtime behavior correct.

Nonsending ecosystem migration audit (Tier 2, v1.0.0) — 26 `isolation:` parameter occurrences across ecosystem. 10 are SE-0421 conformances (KEEP). 14 are deprecated convenience functions (MIGRATE). Double-nonsending pattern applies to 8 witness/dependency functions. All 252 packages have NonisolatedNonsendingByDefault. All 7 AsyncIteratorProtocol + 9 Clock conformances pre-protected.
swift.org +assertions toolchain crashes with ~Escapable/~Copyable (Tier 3) — [#87029](https://github.com/swiftlang/swift/issues/87029), [#87030](https://github.com/swiftlang/swift/issues/87030)

Owned typed memory region abstraction (Tier 3, v2.0) — SLR (17 sources: Capability Calculus, Cyclone, Rust/C++, Resource Polymorphism), formal semantics. Gap: no self-owning typed contiguous region. Decision: `Memory.Contiguous<Element: BitwiseCopyable>` fills the gap in its natural namespace. String.Storage wraps `Memory.Contiguous<Char>` + null-termination. BitwiseCopyable is the formal boundary with Storage (per-element lifecycle).

Ownership transfer: sending, Sendable, nonsending, ~Sendable conventions. Consolidates sending-expansion-audit, tilde-sendable-semantic-inventory, nonsending-ecosystem-migration-audit, nonsending-adoption-audit, nonsending-compiler-patterns, non-sendable-strategy-isolation-design, tagged-structural-sendable, sendable-in-rendering-and-snapshot-infrastructure, callback-isolated-nonsending-design. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

Parser bridge architecture (Tier 2) — bifurcated parsing in standards packages: composed parser combinators in Parse struct files vs ad-hoc byte-level parsing in public APIs. Zero public APIs delegate to parse structs. Investigation of bridge pattern to connect them.

Parser bridge ergonomics assessment (Tier 2) — MediaType exemplar validates parser bridge pattern. 6 friction points identified: ByteInput qualification noise (F-1), dual generic constraint `Collection.Slice.Protocol & Swift.Collection` (F-2, resolved by `Input.Slice: Swift.Collection` infrastructure fix), slice→String conversion ceremony (F-3), missing grammar combinators for `_splitOnComma`/`_trimOWS` (F-4), parser name collision (F-5), dead `_quotedString` helper (F-6). 313 lines of inline parsing across 10 unmigrated domain types. ISO 8601 demonstrates target unified pattern. 5 recommendations: migrate remaining types, add `TokenOrQuotedString` combinator, consider UTF8String wrapper (deferred), delete helpers post-migration, document constraint guidance.

Parser combinator algebraic foundations (Tier 3) — SLR (19 sources, Chomsky–Krishnaswami lineage), formal semantics, two-level algebra analysis (constructor-level vs value-level). Recommends Option D (doc + law tests) over explicit algebra dependency; identifies semiring-parameterized parsing as future work.

Parser syntax and ergonomics comparison (Tier 2) — how Swift Institute parser composition syntax compares to pointfreeco/swift-parsing in ergonomics, readability, and boilerplate. Architecturally stronger (typed throws, ~Copyable, generic Input) but syntactically heavier. Gap largest in error handling, parser definition boilerplate, ad-hoc parsing.

Parser combinator adoption implementation plan (Tier 2) — 8-phase leaf-first execution plan. Phase 0: Layer 1 ASCII integer parser (prerequisite). Phase 1: RFC 9110 shared HTTP parsers (8 reusable types). Phase 3: RFC 3986 URI sub-parsers. ~51 new files, ~67 rewritten files, ~123 parse functions affected. Foundation elimination for RFC 6750, W3C SVG, RFC 9111 CharacterSet leak. Phases 5–7 parallelizable.

Parser combinator adoption audit (Tier 1) — 95 adoption opportunities (52 HIGH) across ~30 standards packages. HTTP headers largest single opportunity (23 findings). ISO 8601 most impactful single rewrite (~400 lines). Foundation elimination possible for RFC 6750, W3C SVG, RFC 9111. Only 2 of ~100+ standards packages currently use parser-primitives.

Parsing and serialization capability organization from first principles (Tier 2, v1.3) — three fundamental capabilities (parsing, serialization, printing) plus one conjunction (coding/ParserPrinter). Three complementary implementation strategies: capability protocols, witness types, domain protocols. Domain namespace principle. Layer distribution. All tensions and open items resolved by transformation-domain-architecture (DECISION), canonical-witness-capability-attachment (DECISION), and ascii-serialization-migration (DECISION).

Case study: swift-pdf-standard (Tier 1, v2.0) — traces the full PDF stack from swift-iso-32000 through swift-pdf. Shape C (standard-first, single-spec): stability value justifies keeping. Leaky abstraction acceptable for single-spec shapes. Evaluate moving 3 rendering types (Configuration, Rectangle, Stroke) to swift-pdf-rendering.

Phantom-typed value wrappers literature study and comparative analysis (Tier 3) — SLR (36 sources: Reynolds/Wadler parametricity, Leijen-Meijer/Fluet-Pucella/Cheney-Hinze phantom types, Breitner et al. Coercible, Tov-Pucella affine types), formal semantics, cross-language comparison (Haskell/Rust/OCaml/TypeScript), Cognitive Dimensions. Validates Tagged design; identifies Swift's noncopyable phantom types as novel contribution.
PR submission research for swiftlang/swift (Tier 2) — fork requirements, GitHub CLI workflow, contributor setup from the coenttb account.

PredictableDeadAllocationElimination spurious destroy_value via lifetime completion (Tier 2) — root cause analysis for swiftlang/swift#88022. Prior hypothesis (InstructionDeleter.forceDelete) refuted; actual mechanism traced through `promoteMarkDepBase` and `fixLifetimes=false`.

Key findings: parser/serializer 10:1 asymmetry, 7 orphaned protocols, 22 isolated packages (Bitset/Predicate/Token richest), 337 empty modules. Property for @CoW Copyable types (Tier 1, v2) — [IMPL-021] is correct as written. Property.View is compiler-rejected for Copyable types. @CoW `_read/_modify` accessors + standard Property `_modify` + defer pattern avoids deep copies without transfer placeholder.

Rendering.Context protocol vs witness vs action dispatch (Tier 2) — empirical performance comparison. V2 Witness ≈ V1 Protocol (0.99–1.04x release). Action 1.20–1.36x. AnyView 1.03–1.07x. Decision: hybrid (keep protocol, add Rendering.Action enum for deferred rendering).

Rendering.Context witness migration implications (Tier 2) — full audit of 4 rendering packages. Blast radius: ~26 files modified, ~533 unaffected. Ownership.Mutable capture (no unsafe pointers). Context transformer pattern for PDF.HTML. Nested Action enum (Push/Pop). All blockers validated by experiment (24 tests).

Rendering.View associated type naming — Body→RenderBody rename to avoid SwiftUI.View.Body collision. 3 options: Content, RenderBody, wrapper. Decision: RenderBody (collision-proof, 27-file blast radius).

Rendering.Context witness architecture value analysis (Tier 2) — protocol→witness migration for Rendering.Context. Performance parity validated (0.99–1.04x). Ownership.Mutable capture, consuming transformers, nested Action enum. 4-phase migration.

Snapshot naming patterns: cross-ecosystem analysis — named vs unnamed snapshots, inline vs file-backed storage, API design across 8 frameworks (swift-snapshot-testing, Jest, Vitest, Insta, cargo-insta, Verify, ApprovalTests.NET, snapshot_testing Ruby). Key finding: no framework supporting both inline and file-backed uses auto-numbering for inline snapshots.

Snapshot testing and inline snapshot testing literature study (Tier 3, v1.1) — SLR (45 sources: Barr oracle taxonomy, Rothermel regression theory, Feathers characterization tests, Myers diff). 14 ecosystems surveyed (Rust insta, OCaml ppx_expect, JS Jest/Vitest, Python inline-snapshot, JVM Selfie, Swift PointFree). Formal semantics (oracle, serializer canonicity, source rewrite, typing rules, soundness theorem). Cognitive Dimensions analysis (inline > file-based on 4 dimensions). Swift 6.2+ enablers (pre-built SwiftSyntax, ST-0009 Attachments, SE-0451 raw identifiers). Infrastructure mapping: optics-primitives (redaction composition algebra), tree-primitives (structural diff via Tree.Keyed), json/xml (canonical serialization), witnesses/dependencies (scoped configuration). 5 priority improvements with revised effort estimates.

Source location unification via primitive decomposition (Tier 2, v3.1) — factors `(line, column)` into `Text.Location`, renames `Source.Location` → `Source.Position` (compact) and `Source.Location.Resolved` → `Source.Location` (display). Fully implemented: eliminated `Test.Source.Location`, `Witness.Unimplemented.Location`, `Parser.Diagnostic.Location`, `JSON.LocatedError` — all use canonical primitives types directly. 7 packages committed.

Span view integration strategy (Tier 3) — how Span should integrate with the Swift Institute sequence ecosystem for closure-free consumption. Span-to-String and Span-to-Array conversion paths. SE-0456/SE-0467/SE-0474 prior art.

SPM nested package publication feasibility (Tier 2) — empirical validation (12 test configurations), prior art survey (10 proposals/pitches, 8 major packages), Swift Forums confirmation. Nested Package.swift rejected (hard SPM limitation). Current three-repo layered model confirmed as only viable architecture. Git subtree evaluation deferred.

Architectural role of swift-*-standard packages after spec implementations split into standards-body orgs (Tier 2, v2.0) — domain shape taxonomy (primitive-first vs standard-first, single-spec vs multi-spec), two purposes (convergence + stability), three-layer model (body orgs → swift-standards → swift-foundations), stable-vs-volatile separation. Recommends keeping all 17 packages in swift-standards/. Withdraws v1.0 "eliminate thin re-exports" recommendation.
Storage and buffer variant abstraction analysis (Tier 3) — SLR (40 sources), container theory, substructural typing, comparative analysis of 5 candidate abstractions

Stream isolation-preserving operators via concrete types (Tier 2) — experiment confirms concrete `AsyncSequence` operator types with `NonisolatedNonsendingByDefault` preserve isolation for sync AND async closures. Recommends ~20 concrete linear operators + existing type-erased concurrent operators.

Stream isolation propagation analysis (Tier 2) — every operator breaks isolation (100% breakage). Root causes: `@Sendable` `_next`, sync closures cannot be nonsending, actor state, unstructured Tasks. Option D: accept stream as concurrency boundary.

String and path type unification across ecosystem (Tier 3, v2.0) — SLR (16 sources), formal semantics, prior art (Rust/C++/Go/Zig/Python/Swift). 5 types across 3 layers. Experiment `phantom-tagged-string-unification` (9 variants, ALL CONFIRMED) **refuted** C2 blocker — Option D is feasible today. Two viable paths: Option E (refine, conservative) or Option D (phantom-tag, progressive). Decision deferred to design judgment.

String_Primitives.String shadowing Swift.String (Tier 2) — @_exported chain propagates bare `String` type through Kernel to IO/File/Async, breaking withTaskGroup type inference. Type and name are legitimate (ecosystem pattern: Array, Dictionary, Set also shadow). Problem is structural: Kernel_Primitives_Core @_exports String_Primitives despite not using it. Recommends removing @_exported from Core + demoting Kernel_String_Primitives from umbrella (~6 file changes). Follow-up: audit all @_exported chains for same pattern.

What Tag for String_Primitives.String under D' migration (Tier 2) — consumer inventory across 3 layers. Resolved: Tag is always the consumer's domain (Kernel, Loader), concrete PlatformString is the RawValue

Swift 6.4-dev compatibility catalog (Tier 2) — systematic catalogue of 6.4-dev compatibility issues in swift-primitives: `@_lifetime` on Escapable rejected, static property resolution changes, identity closure IRGen crashes, `@_lifetime` version skew for ~Escapable self methods, DeinitDevirtualizer SIL assertion. Categorize by fix type and dual-compiler requirements.

Performance testing infrastructure gaps and fixes (Tier 2) — 4 issues blocking reliable performance testing: compiler crash in release (P0), unattributable diagnostics (P1), cross-suite concurrency interference (P2), missing tabular comparison (P3). All resolved.

Converged plan for swift-translating migration — replace Language enum with BCP47.LanguageTag typealias, split Foundation-dependent code into Translating Platform module, strict one-way dependency graph

SwiftPM build plugins for `-Xfrontend` flags (Tier 2) — can build plugins inject `-Xfrontend -enable-experimental-feature` flags without `.unsafeFlags()`? Currently `.unsafeFlags()` disables SPM dependency resolution for downstream consumers, blocking adoption of packages using experimental features (Lifetimes, RawLayout).

System vs Kernel.System namespace boundary (Tier 2) — prior art survey (BSD sysctl CTL_HW/CTL_KERN, Linux sysfs /sys/devices/system/ vs /sys/kernel/, Go, Rust, Apple swift-system). Finding: hardware facts (CPU count, RAM, page size) universally separated from kernel operations; no precedent puts them under "kernel". Recommends Option D: System as complete typed hardware namespace (System.Processor.Count, System.Memory.Capacity, System.Page.Size), Kernel.System retains only OS-operational queries (Name, Path.Length, sleep).
Tagged extension duplication analysis

How to resolve Kernel.Path/String identity when both wrap Memory.Contiguous\<Char\> (Tier 2) — 7 options (A–G). Decision: Option G — concrete types as RawValue, domain as Tag. Kind × Domain = two orthogonal axes. Validated by tagged-two-level-lifetime (6/6 CONFIRMED)
Can Tagged<Element, Cardinal> prove structural Sendable when Element: Sendable? (Tier 1) — could remove @unchecked from phantom-typed containers

Task-local stack unification (Tier 2, v3.0) — structural unification of L1 Dependency.Scope and L3 Witness.Context @TaskLocal stacks. Triggered by architectural dissatisfaction with closure-chain bridging mechanism during Phase 3 L1 Key Bridge implementation.

Temporal operator determinism (Tier 2) — NonsendingClock and deterministic temporal testing, triggered by Pointfree Episode #355. ImmediateClock with nonisolated(nonsending) sleep achieves 100% deterministic testing (10,000 runs, 0 failures).

Test console output: from functional to elegant (Tier 2) — comprehensive analysis of console output across unit tests, performance benchmarks, and snapshot tests. Current state: flat streaming with no hierarchy, no suite aggregation, mixed formatting vocabularies. Apple: experimental AdvancedConsoleOutputRecorder with deferred tree rendering. Prior art: Jest, criterion.rs, hyperfine. Theoretical ideal: 6-phase plan — suite-level aggregation + tree summary (Phase 1), right-aligned durations + terminal width (Phase 2), box-drawn performance tables (Phase 3), real-time suite progress (Phase 4), side-by-side benchmark comparison (Phase 5), comparison CLI (Phase 6). Hybrid reporter architecture (Option C) recommended.

Test output quality: parity with Apple and beyond (Tier 2) — 7 improvements identified. Root cause: ConsoleSink discards all Test.Text styling, ignores Failure.expected/actual/difference/comment, omits source location. All data already captured in primitives types. Phase 1: ANSI color + source location + structured fields in reporter (~60 lines, 1 file). Phase 2: pass DiffResult as Failure.difference instead of string-embedding. Phase 3: type-aware sourceCode. After Phase 1-3: parity on all Apple features, exceeding on structural diffs, semantic styling, extensibility.
Test support snapshot strategy sharing (Tier 2) — cross-package sharing of swift-testing-dependent Test.Snapshot.Strategy extensions between parent and nested test support layers
Testing conventions for Swift Institute ecosystem (Tier 3)

Testing discovery revalidation (Swift 6.2.4) — backticked names, nested suites, deep nesting all work. Generic type extension nesting still broken (#1508). ~653 compound test suite names across ecosystem need renaming. No skill updates required.

Time API naming literature review (Tier 2) — Bloch, Fowler, Muratori, Schankin et al. (ICPC 2018), SE-0329 review threads. Nested accessor vs compound identifier analysis. Empirical gap: no study compares `x.in.seconds` vs `x.inSeconds`

Cross-language time/duration API survey (Tier 2) — 7 languages (Rust, Java, C++, Haskell, Go, Python, Swift stdlib), exact method signatures, prefix taxonomy (`as_`/`to`/`in`/`total`/bare), total-vs-component ambiguity analysis

Transformation domain architecture (Tier 2, v3.2) — three top-level namespaces: Parser, Serializer, Coder. Principled correctness framing. Three packages: swift-parser-primitives (existing), swift-serializer-primitives (renamed from swift-serialization-primitives, + Serializer.Protocol + Builder), swift-coder-primitives (new). Coder.Protocol independent with separate DecodeFailure/EncodeFailure. `decode`/`encode` naming. Witness.Protocol + Dependency.Key orthogonal. Shadow stdlib Codable. Canonical-witness attachment validated (10/10 CONFIRMED). All questions resolved.

Tree type family organization across 7 ecosystems — tree variants never share common Tree protocol. "Tree" always the adjective (TreeMap = Map). GNU PBDS `basic_branch_tag` is sole partial exception. Structural trees vs map-backing trees are separate concerns.

Trie and keyed-tree SLR (Tier 3) — Kitchenham methodology, 34 sources across 7 languages. RQ1: canonical signature `Trie<K,V>` where key = `[K]`. RQ2: tries classified as maps, never trees. RQ3: Hinze exponent-law derivation. RQ4: keyed trees extremely rare (Boost.PropertyTree only). Child representation taxonomy (7 strategies).
Complete typed infrastructure inventory, tiers 0–15 (Tier 3) — systematic catalog for existing-infrastructure skill rebuild

Typed throws mixed error domains (Tier 1) — 4 options for aggregate functions composing multiple typed-throws sub-operations: unified error enum (A), Either/sum type (B), phase-tagged wrapping (C), foreign re-throw (D). 3 concrete sites: rfc-9112 Request/Response deserializers (6 error types each), iso-8601 DateTime (1 foreign Time.Error). Recommends Option A (unified error enum) for all sites — wrapping sub-phase errors as associated values preserves full type safety, readability, layer isolation.

Typed throws standards inventory — exhaustive grep of all 2316 source files across 94 packages. 124 untyped throws: 122 Codable protocol-mandated (not worth converting), 2 Clock conformances (converted). 70 packages with zero untyped throws.

Three blockers investigated: ~Copyable enum associated values, borrowing/consuming parameter forwarding, feature value proposition.

Should swift-witnesses support ~Copyable/~Escapable witness values? (Tier 3, v2.0) — SLR (31 sources: Rust/Haskell/OCaml DI, substructural type theory, capability-passing). ~Copyable values feasible (Ownership.Shared, SuppressedAssociatedTypes, TaskLocal not a blocker) but not yet justified — no concrete use case for ~Copyable witness values. Additive closure-based API (Option E) ready when needed. Monitor ~Escapable scoped access.

Witness framework: bifurcation theorem, Sendable removal, macro omission pattern. Consolidates witness-noncopyable-nonescapable-support, witness-macro-noncopyable-support-design, witness-protocol-sendable-requirement. Tier 2, ecosystem-wide.

Should Witness.Protocol require Sendable? (Tier 1) — Remove. Sendable is orthogonal to the witness pattern marker. All existing conformances already declare Sendable explicitly. Witness.Key has independent Sendable constraint. Enables Parser.Machine.Compile.Witness conformance.

Witness Result/Outcome unification (Tier 1) — analysis of @Witness-generated Calls, Result (~Copyable), and Outcome types. Sibling vs nested placement, naming collisions with Standard_Library_Extensions.Result, type structure for DI after-observers.

Witnesses ecosystem adoption audit (Tier 1, v3.0) — identifies every opportunity to adopt Witness.Protocol conformance, @Witness macro usage, and Witness.Key registration across the entire ecosystem. Currently limited to algebra packages and Sample.Averaging; IO drivers use pattern but don't conform.

Worklist rendering dispatch for PDF HTML pipeline (Tier 2) — replaces mutual recursion (`renderHTMLView ↔ renderInnerContent`, 18 frames) with single `Stack<Dispatch>` worklist loop (5 frames), composing from `swift-stack-primitives` and `swift-tree-primitives` patterns. Machine primitives evaluated and rejected. 8 ad-hoc functions absorbed into 1 loop. Supersedes `swift-foundations/swift-pdf-html-rendering/Research/iterative-tuple-rendering.md` solution (retains its tuple/cause analysis).

Workspace-wide symbol search for CCLSP (Tier 2) — 6 options evaluated. Key findings: `rootMarkers` in cclsp.json is silently ignored (not in schema), CCLSP sends exactly one workspace folder, `.xcworkspace` not supported by SourceKit-LSP. Recommends Phase 1: set `rootDir` (trivial, enables implicit workspace discovery), Phase 2: contribute `workspaceFolders` support to ktnyt/cclsp, Phase 3: lightweight root Package.swift if progressive discovery insufficient.

Zero-copy event pipeline design using Memory.Pool (Tier 2) — eliminates per-poll Array allocation in IO.Event.Poll.Loop via pooled buffer slots. Phased: Phase 1 (pool + memcpy), Phase 2 (direct-to-pool polling).

narrativeSections.heading: Reflections
narrativeSections.body: Post-session reflections are captured via the **reflect-session** skill and processed via **reflections-processing**. They live under [Reflections/](Reflections/); see [Reflections/_index.md](Reflections/_index.md) for the date/title/packages/status table.

narrativeSections.heading: Moved to swift-memory-primitives/Research/
narrativeSections.body:
The following memory/pointer research has been consolidated into `swift-primitives/swift-memory-primitives/Research/`:

- `ordinal-cardinal-foundations.md` — Mathematical foundations for ordinal/cardinal separation
- `affine-scaling-operations.md` — Tier 3 research on scaling operations in affine spaces
- `pointer-architecture-comparison.md` — Swift stdlib pointer vs memory/pointer primitives
- `lifetime-dependent-borrowed-cursors.md` — Lifetime-dependent borrowed cursor patterns

narrativeSections.heading: Workflow
narrativeSections.body: Research workflow is defined in the **research-process** skill (`Skills/research-process/SKILL.md`).
